message:
fix(robot): support directory-scoped glob patterns in matchesPattern

When a glob pattern contains a slash (e.g. "dir/*.go"), normalize
both pattern and path with path.Clean and use path.Match for
accurate directory-scoped matching. Previously, patterns like
"dir/*.go" would incorrectly match "dir/sub/file.go".

Adds test cases for "dir/*.go" (match) and "dir/sub/file.go" against
"dir/*.go" (no match) to verify the new behavior.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -292,6 +292,8 @@ func TestMatchesPattern(t *testing.T) {
 		{"file.go", "*.go", true},
 		{"file.txt", "*.go", false},
 		{"dir/file.go", "*.go", true}, // matches basename
+		{"dir/file.go", "dir/*.go", true},
+		{"dir/sub/file.go", "dir/*.go", false},
 
 		// Directory glob patterns
 		{"internal/robot/file.go", "internal/**", true},
